item.title: kmiri: fix stack resource exhaustion via StackPopAllocTracker
item.body: * Fix https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/60 * Fix https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/61 This PR handles the new stack allocations during stack popping: 1. Each thread now has a StackPopAllocTracker which is used to do stack allocation based on the saved SP in between before_stack_pop and after_stack_pop. 2. StackPopAllocTracker should be considered as isolated caller allocation data, especially with separated int_to_ptr_map; global_state int_to_ptr_map is for callee during the stack popping, so stack deallocation prefers removing the address from global_state. Also * Improves the debugger's stack pane by displaying the last_recorded_stack_ptr. * Revert the physical memory layout in the config. The maximum stack footprint is now only 6MB, a 70% reduction from the original 18.61MB in #15. No stack resource exhaustion. :tada:

repo.name: miri
repo.description: [A forked miri for OS] An interpreter for Rust's mid-level intermediate representation

issue.title: kmiri: fix stack resource exhaustion via StackPopAllocTracker
issue.body: * Fix https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/60 * Fix https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/61 This PR handles the new stack allocations during stack popping: 1. Each thread now has a StackPopAllocTracker which is used to do stack allocation based on the saved SP in between before_stack_pop and after_stack_pop. 2. StackPopAllocTracker should be considered as isolated caller allocation data, especially with separated int_to_ptr_map; global_state int_to_ptr_map is for callee during the stack popping, so stack deallocation prefers removing the address from global_state. Also * Improves the debugger's stack pane by displaying the last_recorded_stack_ptr. * Revert the physical memory layout in the config. The maximum stack footprint is now only 6MB, a 70% reduction from the original 18.61MB in #15. No stack resource exhaustion. :tada:

commit.message: kmiri: fix the condition during the conflict of int_to_ptr_map insertion

kmiri: resume stack point in before_stack_pop

unluckily, it doesn't resolve the allocation overlap problem

kmiri: hard error if multiple AllocIds point to the same address
kmiri: restore the SP and int_to_ptr_map changes

kmiri: record stack alloc and second alloc path during stack pop

主要改动：

- 在 `Thread` 里新增 `stack_pop_allocs`，用于记录 `before_stack_pop` 到 `after_stack_pop` 之间 materialize 出来的栈 allocation，以及这段窗口自己的临时 `next_stack_addr`。对应代码在 https://github.com/KMiri-rs/miri/blob/84ecf058735c82f92e39430fb2870679887a112d/src/concurrency/thread.rs#L188 和 https://github.com/KMiri-rs/miri/blob/84ecf058735c82f92e39430fb2870679887a112d/src/concurrency/thread.rs#L233。

- `MemoryKind::Stack` 分配路径现在会检测是否处于 stack-pop 窗口；如果是，就基于记录里的 caller-side `next_stack_addr` 分配，并按分配发生顺序记录 `AllocId`。对应代码在 https://github.com/KMiri-rs/miri/blob/84ecf058735c82f92e39430fb2870679887a112d/src/alloc_addresses/mod.rs#L271。

- `before_stack_pop` 开始记录窗口，`after_stack_pop` 取出窗口内的新栈 allocation，按 `AllocId` 顺序重新计算 base address，考虑 size/alignment，并同步更新 `base_paddr`、`int_to_ptr_map` 和必要时的 backing allocation/provenance copy。对应代码在 https://github.com/KMiri-rs/miri/blob/84ecf058735c82f92e39430fb2870679887a112d/src/machine.rs#L1162 和 https://github.com/KMiri-rs/miri/blob/84ecf058735c82f92e39430fb2870679887a112d/src/machine.rs#L2152。

实现里额外处理了一个关键边界：窗口内新 allocation 可能先临时撞上尚未 `StorageDead` 的旧 allocation，导致第一次插入 `int_to_ptr_map` 时没有自己的反向映射。现在 `after_stack_pop` 如果发现 old base 已经不在 `int_to_ptr_map`，会记录 `[stack_pop_rebase_missing_old]`，然后把仍 live 的 allocation 按重算后的地址重新插回映射，避免后续 `free_alloc_id` panic。

验证结果：

- `cargo check` 通过。
- `./run.sh` 已运行，原来的 `resource exhaustion: there are no more free addresses in the address space` 没有再出现。
- `miri_debugger.log` 中没有 `AddressSpaceFull`。
- 日志确认该路径大量触发：`stack_pop_alloc` 约 `233232` 次，窗口内分配都被后续 `stack_pop_rebase` 处理。
- 当前 `./run.sh` 仍以 code 6 结束，但已经推进到另一个问题：guest 代码在 https://github.com/KMiri-rs/asterinas/blob/5714a23ba8f0c039c1e829f321a771becf0d365e/ostd/src/mm/page_table/boot_pt.rs#L292 对 `0xffff800001200000[noalloc]` 做 `write_bytes`，Miri 报 dangling pointer/no provenance。这是越过原先栈地址耗尽后的新 UB，不是这次 stack-pop 重排路径的 `AddressSpaceFull`。

Revert "kmiri(mirch): enlarge kernel stack range to 50MB and move it to last"

This reverts commit 26e55ef2da9d930880601924d1d212715c7c33de.
kmiri: debugger_log stack footprint (max stack usage is 1MB now)

Revert "kmiri: debugger_log stack footprint (max stack usage is 1MB now)"

This reverts commit 6653f36588ea0accede6135eb94e1a23f8c30e47.

kmiri: isolate stack-pop temporary address mappings

Use a thread StackPopAllocTracker int_to_ptr_map to avoid the address
collision with the one in global_state int_to_ptr_map.
StackPopAllocTracker should be considered as caller allocation,
so during stack dellocation, remove the matched address from global_state
int_to_ptr_map first as that's callee's allocation.
